content.claim: Between 2020/21 and 2021/22 school years, recorded SEN students in Hungary rose from 92,800 to 97,300; by 2024/2025 the total reached 112,060, representing a 28,000-student increase over ten years, with varying baseline definitions across official reports (cross-level vs. primary-only totals).
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Cross-report comparability is imperfect due to varying definitional scopes (kindergarten–secondary vs. primary-only)
absolute trend magnitude depends on which statistical release is used as baseline
lack of standardized historical series limits rigorous trend decomposition
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
